raw_events.name: Dirty Dozen Brass Band
raw_events.description: GRAMMY-winning New Orleans brass band celebrating 45+ years of fusing traditional New Orleans brass with bebop, funk, and R&B/soul. The band coined the phrase 'musical gumbo.' Teased from issue 3.
raw_events.notes: Was named in last week's teaser. Major national act in a 400-seat room.

raw_events.name: Machinal — A Dance of Defiance
raw_events.description: Dance performance at the URI Fine Arts Center, a defiant retelling.
raw_events.notes: URI event — student/community ticket prices typically modest

raw_events.name: Wakefield Books Author Talk — Linford Fisher & Vanessa Lillie
raw_events.description: Two-author talk featuring historian Linford Fisher and novelist Vanessa Lillie at Wakefield Books on Pier Marketplace.
raw_events.notes: Free or low-cost bookstore event

raw_events.name: The Delightfully Respectful Celebration of the Nation's 50 States
raw_events.description: Dinner-and-presentation event tied to the nation's 250th anniversary. Roberta Mudge Humble of the RI Heritage Hall of Fame presents a tour of the 50 states. Menu: barbecued chicken & pork, salad, mac & cheese, Toll House cookies. Ages 10+.
raw_events.notes: $28 advance reservations required (401.596.8554). Quirky civic dinner.

raw_events.name: Jaws at the Misquamicut Drive-In — Season Opener
raw_events.description: The Misquamicut Drive-In's 2026 season opens with Jaws on the beach. Presented by the Misquamicut Business Association. One ticket per carload — first-come seating. No refunds, no transfers. The first night the drive-in is back is a South County summer ritual.
raw_events.notes: Was named in last week's teaser. Per-car pricing — strong family + nostalgic appeal. Address: 316 Atlantic Ave, Westerly.

raw_events.name: Dance Night at The Knick with Eight to the Bar & UNITED Big Band and Jazz Combo
raw_events.description: Eight to the Bar — swing/boogie-woogie/Motown veterans — share the stage with the UNITED Big Band and Jazz Combo for an album-release dance night at the Knick. Dance floor will be moving.
raw_events.notes: Was named in last week's teaser. Dance Night = the room's full intention.

raw_events.name: Pet Sounds Live!
raw_events.description: A live re-creation of the Beach Boys' Pet Sounds — not a tribute, but a recreation of how the studio recording came together, performed in sequence.
raw_events.notes: Classic-album-night format. Pairs well with Houses of the Holy last week at the Courthouse.

raw_events.name: Van Morrison — Moondance (Tribute)
raw_events.description: Tribute performance celebrating Van Morrison's Moondance — the Courthouse's spring classic-albums series continues.
raw_events.notes: Featured event on the Courthouse homepage

raw_events.name: LaMP (Russ Lawton, Scott Metzger, Ray Paczkowski) at The Ocean Mist
raw_events.description: Heavy-hitter jam-trio booking: Russ Lawton (Soulive/Trey Anastasio Band), Scott Metzger (JRAD/WOLF!), and Ray Paczkowski (Trey Anastasio Band) at the Mist. The kind of musicianship-per-cubic-foot ratio you don't usually get in a beach bar.
raw_events.notes: Cross-referenced via Eventbrite because Ocean Mist's own calendar didn't render. Phish/TAB-adjacent personnel — will draw a regional crowd.

raw_events.name: Painting Workshop — Stars & Stripes by the Sea
raw_events.description: Adult painting workshop with a patriotic-coastal theme.
raw_events.notes: Likely sip-and-paint format

raw_events.name: Annual 'Quality Seconds' Pottery and Art Sale
raw_events.description: Discount sale of slightly-imperfect (or beautifully-imperfect) pottery and artwork from SCAA member artists. Once-a-year clearance — collectors line up.
raw_events.notes: Address: 2587 Kingstown Rd. Mother's Day make-up gift / wedding-season material.

raw_events.name: Poppy Champlin's Hot Mic Comedy feat. Stacy Kendro
raw_events.description: Stand-up comedy night hosted by Poppy Champlin, featuring Stacy Kendro. $20.
raw_events.notes: Was teased generically last week; Stacy Kendro confirmed as the feature.

raw_events.name: Mark Cutler & Men of Great Courage
raw_events.description: Late-night set from RI rock veteran Mark Cutler and Men of Great Courage. $15 advance / $20 door.
raw_events.notes: Cutler is a Schemers-era RI rock institution.

raw_events.name: Local Author Niles Major Book Signing
raw_events.description: Regional author Niles Major presents and signs.
raw_events.notes: 160 Old Tower Hill Rd

raw_events.name: Newport Oyster & Chowder Festival
raw_events.description: Freshly shucked RI oysters and award-winning chowders from across the state's farms and kitchens. Live music, signature cocktails, open-air on the wharf.
raw_events.notes: Not South County but the kind of foodie weekend that warrants a 'worth the drive' callout.

raw_events.name: The Wailers — 50 Years of Positive Vibrations (Rastaman Vibration)
raw_events.description: The Wailers honor Bob Marley & The Wailers' Rastaman Vibration with a full set of timeless classics. Local opener.
raw_events.notes: Black Box is intimate — 200 seats — for a band with a 50-year history. Big draw.

raw_events.name: The Wallflowers — Bringing Down the Horse 30th Anniversary
raw_events.description: Jakob Dylan & The Wallflowers perform Bringing Down the Horse in full for its 30th anniversary, with Lost Leaders opening. SOLD OUT.
raw_events.notes: SOLD OUT — don't put in lineup, but worth mentioning in Signal Noise / 'you missed it' framing.

raw_events.name: Donna Byrne Quartet
raw_events.description: Donna Byrne, longtime New England jazz vocalist, in a quartet setting at the Pump House. $20.
raw_events.notes: Jazz Sunday — quieter listening room

raw_events.name: RI Grown Farmers Market at Fishermen's Memorial
raw_events.description: Weekly Sunday farmers market at Fishermen's Memorial — produce, seafood, baked goods, flowers, dairy. Runs May through October.
raw_events.notes: Recurring weekly — consider whether to keep featuring or move to a permanent 'Sunday' bullet

raw_events.name: Berry Bowl Clay Class
raw_events.description: Pottery class focused on building a hand-thrown berry bowl — timed to strawberry season.
raw_events.notes: Could pair with a strawberry-season note

raw_events.name: Roots Music Stomp — Bob Dylan 85th Birthday Bash
raw_events.description: The monthly Roots Music Stomp series goes all-Dylan to celebrate his 85th birthday. Gallery space — casual setup, drinks, local players running through the catalog.
raw_events.notes: Dylan's actual birthday is May 24, but this is the closest Stomp night.

manual_additions_note: pipeline/manual-additions.json still contains the example Shelter Harbor placeholder from March 19. No real forced items applied this week.
editorial_notes: The teaser from issue 3 named three things: Misquamicut Drive-In Jaws opener, Eight to the Bar at the Knick, Dirty Dozen Brass Band at the Odeum. All three are confirmed for this week. The drafter should pay off the teaser in the opener.

Three real headliner candidates compete for the slot: Dirty Dozen Brass Band (Thu), Misquamicut Drive-In opening night with Jaws (Fri), and Eight to the Bar Dance Night at the Knick (Fri). The drive-in is the most South-County-specific moment of the week.

Friday May 15 is dramatically over-stacked: Drive-In, Eight to the Bar at Knick, LaMP at Ocean Mist, Pet Sounds at Odeum, Van Morrison tribute at Courthouse. Consider framing as a 'pick your Friday' moment like issue 3 did.
The Wallflowers (Sun 5/17) is SOLD OUT — don't put in lineup, but mentioning it adds 'you needed this newsletter sooner' urgency. Signal Noise material.
LaMP at Ocean Mist is the most surprising booking — Phish/Soulive personnel in a beach bar. Worth featuring even though the venue's own calendar didn't render.

upcoming_notable.name: Memorial Day Weekend (May 22-25)
upcoming_notable.notes: Tease the full Memorial Day weekend — drive-in's second week, beach season opens, Block Island ferry ramps up.
